D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Coma — Off-Axis Comet-Tail PSF

Off-axis rays of different aperture zones land at different positions and magnifications; the result is a comet-shaped PSF that gets worse with field angle

For a lens with coma coefficient C, an off-axis point at angle θ_f produces a PSF whose tail length grows as θ_f. The Abbe sine condition (n sin u = const) eliminates coma at one zone — used in microscope objectives and telescope correctors (Ritchey-Chrétien).

Slide field angle to see PSF spread; aperture rings map to comet tail segments.
